title: Delivery status uses magic strings
description:
src/delivery.rs and src/db.rs — Delivery queue status is stored and queried as magic strings ('pending', 'dead') throughout the codebase:

- db.rs:55 — DEFAULT 'pending' in schema
- delivery.rs:133 — WHERE dq.status = 'pending'
- delivery.rs:348 — SET status = 'dead'
- delivery.rs:409 — WHERE status = 'dead' / SET status = 'pending'
- delivery.rs:424,429 — WHERE status = 'pending' / WHERE status = 'dead'
- cli.rs:250,256 — WHERE status = 'pending' / WHERE status = 'dead'

A DeliveryStatus enum {Pending, Dead} with Display/FromStr (or sqlx::Type) would catch typos at compile time. Currently a typo like 'pendig' would silently match zero rows.

Impact: No compile-time safety for status values. A typo in any of these 10+ locations would be a silent bug.
close_reason: SQLite stores TEXT — Rust enum would need string conversions at every query. Constants add no safety since SQL queries use literals.
